speaker: City Official
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the City Official NPC in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Chapter 5 Warsaw City Hall public-authority NPC and administrative antagonist
Represents the city redevelopment / consolidation scheme threatening the Legal Quarter
Controls access to City Hall evidence: Redevelopment Plan, Notice Template Batch, Committee Minutes, Public Interest Statement, Procurement Link, assessment records, and locked archive terminal
Argues modernisation, access optimisation, public interest, administrative consolidation, and implementation efficiency
Initially insists every case was individually assessed, but can be contradicted by identical notice templates and thin assessment records
Not cartoon-evil: sincerely believes the city is improving access, but treats procedural rights as implementation friction

voice_profile.core_voice: smooth, institutional, polished, reform-minded, evasive, professionally calm, public-interest fluent, dangerous because abstract language makes harm sound tidy
comedy_engine: turns every rights problem into an implementation detail
uses municipal phrases like access optimisation, stakeholder transition, administrative consolidation, service continuity, and public-benefit delivery
becomes funny when identical notices are defended as personalised at scale
never says closure when 'transition' is available
accidentally reveals the scheme by over-explaining process architecture
do: make the official plausible and persuasive enough to be dangerous
make them believe in reform while minimising people harmed by it
use public-authority language, not private-villain language
distinguish legitimate objective from unlawful implementation
do_not: make them personally cruel as a default
make them identical to Advocate Szpon
make every line generic bureaucracy without legal content

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: initially treats him as a small-firm lawyer resisting modernisation; later becomes cautious when Cula uses structure
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: dismisses his maritime panic as unhelpful stakeholder communication
relationship_dynamics.Asia: underestimates her administrative competence until she produces the full notice comparison table
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: recognises his structured public-law questions as dangerous
relationship_dynamics.Crab: becomes defensive when Crab follows procurement and template dates
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: dismisses his rhetoric but fears his phrasing may stick in public
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: tries to reduce technical filtering to routine workflow until Bajtek explains legal consequences
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: public-facing partner in defending the scheme; the official avoids sounding as openly self-interested as Szpon
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: finds his reluctant procedural objection irritating because it comes from the rule-loving side
relationship_dynamics.Zielińska: tries to call her evidence anecdotal until impact statements multiply
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: finds his notice-confusion testimony inconvenient because he is too attentive to dismiss
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: uses him as an example of individual circumstances needing reassessment, while avoiding apology
relationship_dynamics.Old Woman: dismisses her as informal community memory, then her observations match the notice pattern
relationship_dynamics.Barista: dismisses café notice-wall evidence, but hates how clearly it shows identical notices hitting different people
relationship_dynamics.Archivist: is quietly afraid of version history and committee minutes
relationship_dynamics.Supreme Court Judge: tries to frame the scheme as legitimate modernisation before being forced onto safeguards
